reason: Stale at decision time. Target skills/orchestration_audit/verify.py was hand-built at commit b7aa043 (orchestration_audit: 0.2 v0). The hand-built verifier is behavioral (walks each collector's dataset for 'all live', runs trace_conformity_probes, checks pfsm_parameters freshness against compute_source_state); the structural-only candidate would be a regression in coverage for a 0.2-signal-carrying target. The promoter would have refused with exit code 3 (target exists). Logged as reject so the proposal does not re-surface in REVIEW.md.

First promotion. Target is the prior-art skill the experiment rides under (skills/subprotocol-for-claude-code). Structural verification (presence + parse + LLM-SDK-deny + nondeterminism-deny) is shape-fit: the bundle is heterogeneous prose+yaml+scripts and carries no 0.2 signal whose claim a behavioral probe would need to exercise. All 7 NAMED_FILES verified present pre-decision. Substituted for the stale orchestration_audit proposal. Pre-verification per proposals/REVIEW.md: 7/7 checks pass, audit budget 74/80.

reason_pointer.excerpt: if I say yes this dashboard becomes 0.4 candidate I am telling you that is awful this dashboard sucks it is OK as a prototype it has no generative UI it does not have a Cam board we do not have visualizations this is hardly a 0.1 ... denied rejected ... old school bad code unthought out not the point of what 0.4 is and we should really take a red flag

schema_note: New shape: transcript-pointer-anchored. Replaces the prior {by, reason} prose fields. The transcript pointer is the structural authorization receipt; the excerpt is computed-at-write-time from the transcript line, not authored prose.
